name: gha-concurrency-cycle
description: Detect static GitHub Actions reusable-workflow concurrency deadlocks before they cancel a run.
targetUsers: Platform engineers and repository maintainers using same-repository reusable GitHub Actions workflows with workflow-level concurrency.
jobToBeDone: Detect statically resolvable caller and called-workflow effective concurrency-group collisions before a push causes cancellation or a wait cycle.
distributionPath: Checksum-verified reproducible Linux and macOS archives, go install, and a source-built composite GitHub Action using the same CLI contract.
successMetric: Within 30 days, one unrelated external repository uses the tool and directly reports or demonstrates a real collision found before merge and fixed with caller-only ownership.

demandEvidence.independenceKey: Pepabo monorepo CI orchestration migration team
github gh-aw reusable workflow cancellation project

alternatives.name: GitHub reusable workflow concurrency documentation
alternatives.gap: Documents the caller cancellation mechanism but does not preflight a repository workflow graph before push.

alternatives.name: actionlint 1.7.12
alternatives.gap: Passed both the validated collision and safe fixtures without distinguishing the caller-context effective group cycle.

alternatives.name: zizmor 1.27.0
alternatives.gap: Provides broad GitHub Actions security analysis but did not distinguish the validated collision and safe fixtures.

alternatives.name: act 0.2.89
alternatives.gap: Runs workflows locally but did not expose GitHub service-side caller concurrency-group resolution for the fixture pair.

duplicateSearch.summary: Compared GitHub documentation and installed actionlint, zizmor, and act against the same collision and safe fixtures; none produced the bounded GCC001 distinction.

differentiation: A read-only offline analyzer for only statically certain same-repository caller-context workflow-level concurrency cycles, with deterministic text and JSON diagnostics.
